ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-05-2022 Continued To Oct-19-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brian Wagner, via Zoom, for Thomas Dubberke, Executor.

Counsel reports regarding the delay in preparing a petition and advises that a Petition for Final Distribution is pending in the court's e-filing queue.

The Status Hearing is continued to Oct-19-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204 to allow for the filing of the pending petition. Counsel waives notice.

Judge: Hon. William Barry. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Saephan. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-21-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Dubberke appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

Mr. Dubberke stated that he is a civil legal research attorney for the Alameda County Superior Court, that he was a civil litigator for 19 years prior, that he and his brother David are the only descendants, that his father has $64,000.00 in a Wells Fargo Bank account and other checks pending, and that it is his understanding that the estate will go 50/50. Mr. Dubberke also stated that the main asset is a condo on Fell Street in San Francisco, that no repairs have been made in twenty-five years, and that they need to fix the place up for sale and split the proceeds between he and his brother. In addition, Mr. Dubberke stated that tax returns for 2020 and 2021 have not been submitted, that Louise is his mother and his father's ex-wife, that they were divorced in 1986 and that he has never administered an estate. The court noted that Louise is mentioned in the will as a possible executor. Mr. Dubberke stated that he is aware that he has to keep his dad's assets separate from his own assets, that there are to be no distributions without a court order, that he needs to keep cash funds in an insured account and that he has a fiduciary duty to the beneficiaries. Mr. Dubberke also stated that his brother has not signed a consent, that he understands that the court may order a bond at any time, that his bar card is active, that he will hire an attorney if he needs to and that he will try and handle everything on his own. The court informed Mr. Dubberke that there are document preparers that can provide assistance and that there are requirements that he needs to follow to sell the property. There being no objections and the petition being duly noticed, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated January 30, 1988 is admitted to probate. Thomas Dubberke is appointed as the Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is set for February 8, 2022 at 9:00 a. m., in Dept. 204. Status on close of estate is set for October 05, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
